bugs.root_cause: CleanStale used syscall.Stat_t and os.Getuid inline. syscall.Stat_t is not defined on Windows, so cross-compilation for GOOS=windows fails at the build step. Local `go build` on Linux compiled fine and hid the problem; the release workflow cross-compiles for six targets and caught it.
bugs.fix: Extracted the ownership check into a platform-specific helper isOwnedByCurrentUser(info). Added cli/internal/sandbox/ownership_unix.go (build tag !windows) with the real syscall.Stat_t/os.Getuid check, and cli/internal/sandbox/ownership_windows.go (build tag windows) that returns true (Windows uses per-user temp paths by default). Removed syscall import from staging.go.

bugs.root_cause: findProjectRootImpl walks up from cwd looking for go.mod/.git/etc. Test chdirs to t.TempDir() under /tmp and expects no markers, but an orphan /tmp/go.mod (module 'scratch', from another tool) silently satisfied the walk-up and suppressed the expected fallback warning.
bugs.fix: Removed orphan /tmp/go.mod to clear ambient state. Filed syllago-xpj40 to make the test isolate properly (stop-walk boundary or HOME-rooted temp dir) so it cannot regress when /tmp is polluted.
